ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (researcher, data analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the project */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the project was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the project was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project that the dataset belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the dataset was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the dataset was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset that the demographic data belongs to */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual */,
  "education" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Number of years of education completed */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the individual */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the individual */,
  "relationship" TEXT /* Relationship status of the individual */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race of the individual */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the individual */,
  "capital_gain" INTEGER /* Capital gain of the individual */,
  "capital_loss" INTEGER /* Capital loss of the individual */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Number of hours worked per week */,
  "native_country" TEXT /* Native country of the individual */,
  "income" TEXT /* Income level of the individual (<=50K or >50K) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the demographic data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the demographic data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project that the model belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Machine learning algorithm used by the model */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model that made the prediction */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data that was used to make the prediction */,
  "predicted_income" TEXT /* Predicted income level */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the prediction was made */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the prediction was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project that the visualization belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the visualization */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the visualization */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g. bar chart, scatter plot) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the visualization was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the visualization was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action that was performed (e.g. created project, updated model) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
